docket_entries.description: NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF

LAW AND MOTION 302, EX-PARTE APPLICATION FOR A TEMPORARY PROTECTIVE ORDER IS DENIED - BEFORE RULING ON THE EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR WRIT OF ATTACHMENT/TEMPORARY PROTECTIVE ORDER, JUDGE MILLER DISCLOSED TO COUNSEL (COUNSEL FOR URS IN THE COURTROOM AND COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT ON COURT CALL) THAT WHEN SHE WAS A NEW ASSOCIATE AT A LAW FIRM AT THE END OF 1981 AND IN 1982 SHE DID LEGAL WORK FOR AN ENTITY NAMED URS IN CONNECTION WITH A COMPLETELY UNRELATED LITIGATION MATTER CONCERNING AN INTERNAL EMPLOYEE EMBEZZLEMENT. JUDGE MILLER STATED THAT THIS LEGAL REPRESENTATION MORE THAN 30 YEARS AGO DID NOT AFFECT HER ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL IN THIS UNRELATED CASE. JUDGE MILLER GAVE COUNSEL AND OPPORTUNITY TO ASK HER ANY QUESTIONS ABOUT HER DISCLOSURE.

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Feb-14-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JAMES T. JACKSON OF THE MERHAB ROBINSON & CLARKSON LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (714) 972-2333 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT UNIVERSAL MOLDING EXTRUSION CO., IN.C;
ATTY. PAUL R. JOHNSON OF KING & SPALDING, TEL. NO. 318-1200 SPECIALLY APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT TERVITA CORPORATION.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANT TERVITA CORPORATION'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION AND FOR IMPROPER SERVICE - The motion to quash is granted and cross-defendant Tervita Corporation is dismissed. First, plaintiff fails to establish minimum contacts to support a finding of general or specific jurisdiction. Tervita Corporation is a Canadian entity that was retained to perform work in Canada. Second, plaintiff fails to show that it properly served Tervita Corporation's agent for service of process. Third, the Court denies UMEX et al.'s request for jurisdictional discovery. UMEX et al. do not suggest that additional discovery will reveal additional California contacts and allowing the discovery will lead to another trial continuance. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)
